changes: SwarmClaw shipped v1.1.3, v1.1.4, and v1.1.5 in quick succession on 2026-03-16, keeping the control-plane / orchestration lane in active release mode.
ZeroClaw continued its rapid Rust cadence with three fresh v0.3.4-beta cuts (.230, .231, .232), signaling iterative hardening rather than a new stable milestone.
PicoClaw published another nightly build on 2026-03-16, reinforcing that the low-cost edge-device lane is still being actively maintained.
OpenClaw showed fresh repo activity while its latest stable remained v2026.3.13-1; the reference project is still moving, but without a new category-shaping release today.
Hermes remained canonically tracked at NousResearch/hermes-agent, and the monitored set and taxonomy did not need a change.
whyItMatters: SwarmClaw's release burst is the clearest sign today that the ecosystem is not only cloning the assistant runtime, but also building operator layers above it.
ZeroClaw and PicoClaw continuing to ship supports the broader thesis that the lightweight / low-resource lane is still the most execution-heavy branch of the ecosystem.
Because no new project forced a watchlist, taxonomy, or strategic-frame update, today's movement is best read as ongoing product execution rather than a new market turn.
